# decimal

```go
import "github.com/shopspring/decimal"
```

Package decimal implements an arbitrary precision fixed\-point decimal.

The zero\-value of a Decimal is 0, as you would expect.

The best way to create a new Decimal is to use decimal.NewFromString, ex:

```
n, err := decimal.NewFromString("-123.4567")
n.String() // output: "-123.4567"
```

To use Decimal as part of a struct:

```
type StructName struct {
    Number Decimal
}
```

Note: This can "only" represent numbers with a maximum of 2^31 digits after the decimal point.

## Variables

<a name="AvoidScientificNotation"></a>AvoidScientificNotation specifies whether scientific notation should be used when decimal is turned into a string that has a "negative" precision.

For example, 1200 rounded to the nearest 100 cannot accurately be shown as "1200" because the last two digits are unknown. With this set to false, that number would be expressed as "1.2E3" instead.

```go
var AvoidScientificNotation = true
```

<a name="DivisionPrecision"></a>DivisionPrecision is the number of decimal places in the result when it doesn't divide exactly.

Example:

```
d1 := decimal.NewFromFloat(2).Div(decimal.NewFromFloat(3))
d1.String() // output: "0.6666666666666667"
d2 := decimal.NewFromFloat(2).Div(decimal.NewFromFloat(30000))
d2.String() // output: "0.0000666666666667"
d3 := decimal.NewFromFloat(20000).Div(decimal.NewFromFloat(3))
d3.String() // output: "6666.6666666666666667"
decimal.DivisionPrecision = 3
d4 := decimal.NewFromFloat(2).Div(decimal.NewFromFloat(3))
d4.String() // output: "0.667"
```

```go
var DivisionPrecision = 16
```

<a name="ExpMaxIterations"></a>ExpMaxIterations specifies the maximum number of iterations needed to calculate precise natural exponent value using ExpHullAbrham method.

```go
var ExpMaxIterations = 1000
```

<a name="MarshalJSONWithoutQuotes"></a>MarshalJSONWithoutQuotes should be set to true if you want the decimal to be JSON marshaled as a number, instead of as a string. WARNING: this is dangerous for decimals with many digits, since many JSON unmarshallers \(ex: Javascript's\) will unmarshal JSON numbers to IEEE 754 double\-precision floating point numbers, which means you can potentially silently lose precision.

```go
var MarshalJSONWithoutQuotes = false
```

<a name="PowPrecisionNegativeExponent"></a>PowPrecisionNegativeExponent specifies the maximum precision of the result \(digits after decimal point\) when calculating decimal power. Only used for cases where the exponent is a negative number. This constant applies to Pow, PowInt32 and PowBigInt methods, PowWithPrecision method is not constrained by it.

Example:

```
d1, err := decimal.NewFromFloat(15.2).PowInt32(-2)
d1.String() // output: "0.0043282548476454"

decimal.PowPrecisionNegativeExponent = 24
d2, err := decimal.NewFromFloat(15.2).PowInt32(-2)
d2.String() // output: "0.004328254847645429362881"
```

```go
var PowPrecisionNegativeExponent = 16
```

<a name="TrimTrailingZeros"></a>TrimTrailingZeros specifies whether trailing zeroes should be trimmed from a string representation of decimal. If set to true, trailing zeroes will be truncated \(2.00 \-\> 2, 3.11 \-\> 3.11, 13.000 \-\> 13\), otherwise trailing zeroes will be preserved \(2.00 \-\> 2.00, 3.11 \-\> 3.11, 13.000 \-\> 13.000\). Setting this value to false can be useful for APIs where exact decimal string representation matters.

```go
var TrimTrailingZeros = true
```

<a name="Zero"></a>Zero constant, to make computations faster. Zero should never be compared with == or \!= directly, please use decimal.Equal or decimal.Cmp instead.

```go
var Zero = Decimal{}
```

<a name="RescalePair"></a>

## func [RescalePair](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L2051>)

```go
func RescalePair(d1 Decimal, d2 Decimal) (Decimal, Decimal)
```

RescalePair rescales two decimals to common exponential value \(minimal exp of both decimals\)

<a name="Decimal"></a>

## type [Decimal](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L101-L110>)

Decimal represents a fixed\-point decimal. It is immutable. number = value \* 10 ^ exp

```go
type Decimal struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Avg"></a>

### func [Avg](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L2044>)

```go
func Avg(first Decimal, rest ...Decimal) Decimal
```

Avg returns the average value of the provided first and rest Decimals

<a name="Max"></a>

### func [Max](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L2023>)

```go
func Max(first Decimal, rest ...Decimal) Decimal
```

Max returns the largest Decimal that was passed in the arguments.

To call this function with an array, you must do:

```
Max(arr[0], arr[1:]...)
```

This makes it harder to accidentally call Max with 0 arguments.

<a name="Min"></a>

### func [Min](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L2006>)

```go
func Min(first Decimal, rest ...Decimal) Decimal
```

Min returns the smallest Decimal that was passed in the arguments.

To call this function with an array, you must do:

```
Min(arr[0], arr[1:]...)
```

This makes it harder to accidentally call Min with 0 arguments.

<a name="New"></a>

### func [New](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L120>)

```go
func New(value int64, exp int32) Decimal
```

New returns a new fixed\-point decimal, value \* 10 ^ exp.

<a name="NewFromBigInt"></a>

### func [NewFromBigInt](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L166>)

```go
func NewFromBigInt(value *big.Int, exp int32) Decimal
```

NewFromBigInt returns a new Decimal from a big.Int, value \* 10 ^ exp

<a name="NewFromBigRat"></a>

### func [NewFromBigRat](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L182>)

```go
func NewFromBigRat(value *big.Rat, precision int32) Decimal
```

NewFromBigRat returns a new Decimal from a big.Rat. The numerator and denominator are divided and rounded to the given precision.

Example:

```
d1 := NewFromBigRat(big.NewRat(0, 1), 0)    // output: "0"
d2 := NewFromBigRat(big.NewRat(4, 5), 1)    // output: "0.8"
d3 := NewFromBigRat(big.NewRat(1000, 3), 3) // output: "333.333"
d4 := NewFromBigRat(big.NewRat(2, 7), 4)    // output: "0.2857"
```

<a name="NewFromFloat"></a>

### func [NewFromFloat](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L326>)

```go
func NewFromFloat(value float64) Decimal
```

NewFromFloat converts a float64 to Decimal.

The converted number will contain the number of significant digits that can be represented in a float with reliable roundtrip. This is typically 15 digits, but may be more in some cases. See https://www.exploringbinary.com/decimal-precision-of-binary-floating-point-numbers/ for more information.

For slightly faster conversion, use NewFromFloatWithExponent where you can specify the precision in absolute terms.

NOTE: this will panic on NaN, \+/\-inf

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
fmt.Println(NewFromFloat(123.123123123123).String())
fmt.Println(NewFromFloat(.123123123123123).String())
fmt.Println(NewFromFloat(-1e13).String())
// OUTPUT:
//123.123123123123
//0.123123123123123
//-10000000000000
```

#### Output

```
123.123123123123
0.123123123123123
-10000000000000
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewFromFloat32"></a>

### func [NewFromFloat32](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L343>)

```go
func NewFromFloat32(value float32) Decimal
```

NewFromFloat32 converts a float32 to Decimal.

The converted number will contain the number of significant digits that can be represented in a float with reliable roundtrip. This is typically 6\-8 digits depending on the input. See https://www.exploringbinary.com/decimal-precision-of-binary-floating-point-numbers/ for more information.

For slightly faster conversion, use NewFromFloatWithExponent where you can specify the precision in absolute terms.

NOTE: this will panic on NaN, \+/\-inf

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
fmt.Println(NewFromFloat32(123.123123123123).String())
fmt.Println(NewFromFloat32(.123123123123123).String())
fmt.Println(NewFromFloat32(-1e13).String())
// OUTPUT:
//123.12312
//0.123123124
//-10000000000000
```

#### Output

```
123.12312
0.123123124
-10000000000000
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewFromFloatWithExponent"></a>

### func [NewFromFloatWithExponent](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L404>)

```go
func NewFromFloatWithExponent(value float64, exp int32) Decimal
```

NewFromFloatWithExponent converts a float64 to Decimal, with an arbitrary number of fractional digits.

Example:

```
NewFromFloatWithExponent(123.456, -2).String() // output: "123.46"
```

<a name="NewFromFormattedString"></a>

### func [NewFromFormattedString](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L292>)

```go
func NewFromFormattedString(value string, replRegexp *regexp.Regexp) (Decimal, error)
```

NewFromFormattedString returns a new Decimal from a formatted string representation. The second argument \- replRegexp, is a regular expression that is used to find characters that should be removed from given decimal string representation. All matched characters will be replaced with an empty string.

Example:

```
r := regexp.MustCompile("[$,]")
d1, err := NewFromFormattedString("$5,125.99", r)

r2 := regexp.MustCompile("[_]")
d2, err := NewFromFormattedString("1_000_000", r2)

r3 := regexp.MustCompile("[USD\\s]")
d3, err := NewFromFormattedString("5000 USD", r3)
```

<a name="NewFromInt"></a>

### func [NewFromInt](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L133>)

```go
func NewFromInt(value int64) Decimal
```

NewFromInt converts an int64 to Decimal.

Example:

```
NewFromInt(123).String() // output: "123"
NewFromInt(-10).String() // output: "-10"
```

<a name="NewFromInt32"></a>

### func [NewFromInt32](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L146>)

```go
func NewFromInt32(value int32) Decimal
```

NewFromInt32 converts an int32 to Decimal.

Example:

```
NewFromInt(123).String() // output: "123"
NewFromInt(-10).String() // output: "-10"
```

<a name="NewFromString"></a>

### func [NewFromString](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L200>)

```go
func NewFromString(value string) (Decimal, error)
```

NewFromString returns a new Decimal from a string representation. Trailing zeroes are not trimmed.

Example:

```
d, err := NewFromString("-123.45")
d2, err := NewFromString(".0001")
d3, err := NewFromString("1.47000")
```

<a name="NewFromUint64"></a>

### func [NewFromUint64](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L158>)

```go
func NewFromUint64(value uint64) Decimal
```

NewFromUint64 converts an uint64 to Decimal.

Example:

```
NewFromUint64(123).String() // output: "123"
```

<a name="RequireFromString"></a>

### func [RequireFromString](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L308>)

```go
func RequireFromString(value string) Decimal
```

RequireFromString returns a new Decimal from a string representation or panics if NewFromString had returned an error.

Example:

```
d := RequireFromString("-123.45")
d2 := RequireFromString(".0001")
```

```go
func Sum(first Decimal, rest ...Decimal) Decimal
```

<a name="Decimal.Ceil"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Ceil](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1737>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Ceil() Decimal
```

<a name="Decimal.Cmp"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Cmp](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1313>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Cmp(d2 Decimal) int
```

Cmp compares the numbers represented by d and d2 and returns:

```
-1 if d <  d2
 0 if d == d2
+1 if d >  d2
```

<a name="Decimal.Coefficient"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Coefficient](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1406>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Coefficient() *big.Int
```

Coefficient returns the coefficient of the decimal. It is scaled by 10^Exponent\(\)

<a name="Decimal.CoefficientInt64"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [CoefficientInt64](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1413>)

```go
func (d Decimal) CoefficientInt64() int64
```

CoefficientInt64 returns the coefficient of the decimal as int64. It is scaled by 10^Exponent\(\) If coefficient cannot be represented in an int64, the result will be undefined.

<a name="Decimal.Compare"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Compare](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1328>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Compare(d2 Decimal) int
```

Compare compares the numbers represented by d and d2 and returns:

```
-1 if d <  d2
 0 if d == d2
+1 if d >  d2
```

<a name="Decimal.Div"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Div](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L608>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Div(d2 Decimal) Decimal
```

Div returns d / d2. If it doesn't divide exactly, the result will have DivisionPrecision digits after the decimal point.

<a name="Decimal.DivRound"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [DivRound](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L664>)

```go
func (d Decimal) DivRound(d2 Decimal, precision int32) Decimal
```

DivRound divides and rounds to a given precision i.e. to an integer multiple of 10^\(\-precision\)

```
for a positive quotient digit 5 is rounded up, away from 0
if the quotient is negative then digit 5 is rounded down, away from 0
```

Note that precision\<0 is allowed as input.

<a name="Decimal.EncodeSpanner"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [EncodeSpanner](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1911>)

```go
func (d Decimal) EncodeSpanner() (interface{}, error)
```

EncodeSpanner encodes a Decimal into a Spanner value

<a name="Decimal.Equal"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Equal](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1333>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Equal(d2 Decimal) bool
```

Equal returns whether the numbers represented by d and d2 are equal.

<a name="Decimal.Equals"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Equals](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1338>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Equals(d2 Decimal) bool
```

Deprecated: Equals is deprecated, please use Equal method instead.

<a name="Decimal.ExpHullAbrham"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [ExpHullAbrham](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L973>)

```go
func (d Decimal) ExpHullAbrham(overallPrecision uint32) (Decimal, error)
```

ExpHullAbrham calculates the natural exponent of decimal \(e to the power of d\) using Hull\-Abraham algorithm. OverallPrecision argument specifies the overall precision of the result \(integer part \+ decimal part\).

ExpHullAbrham is faster than ExpTaylor for small precision values, but it is much slower for large precision values.

Example:

```
NewFromFloat(26.1).ExpHullAbrham(2).String()    // output: "220000000000"
NewFromFloat(26.1).ExpHullAbrham(20).String()   // output: "216314672147.05767284"
```

<a name="Decimal.ExpTaylor"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [ExpTaylor](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1070>)

```go
func (d Decimal) ExpTaylor(precision int32) (Decimal, error)
```

ExpTaylor calculates the natural exponent of decimal \(e to the power of d\) using Taylor series expansion. Precision argument specifies how precise the result must be \(number of digits after decimal point\). Negative precision is allowed.

ExpTaylor is much faster for large precision values than ExpHullAbrham.

Example:

```
d, err := NewFromFloat(26.1).ExpTaylor(2).String()
d.String()  // output: "216314672147.06"

NewFromFloat(26.1).ExpTaylor(20).String()
d.String()  // output: "216314672147.05767284062928674083"

NewFromFloat(26.1).ExpTaylor(-10).String()
d.String()  // output: "220000000000"
```

<a name="Decimal.Exponent"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Exponent](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1401>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Exponent() int32
```

Exponent returns the exponent, or scale component of the decimal.

<a name="Decimal.Float64"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Float64](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1453>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Float64() (f float64, exact bool)
```

Float64 returns the nearest float64 value for d and a bool indicating whether f represents d exactly. For more details, see the documentation for big.Rat.Float64

<a name="Decimal.Floor"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Floor](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1722>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Floor() Decimal
```

Floor returns the nearest integer value less than or equal to d.

<a name="Decimal.GobDecode"></a>

### func \(\*Decimal\) [GobDecode](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1901>)

```go
func (d *Decimal) GobDecode(data []byte) error
```

GobDecode implements the gob.GobDecoder interface for gob serialization.

<a name="Decimal.GobEncode"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [GobEncode](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1896>)

```go
func (d Decimal) GobEncode() ([]byte, error)
```

GobEncode implements the gob.GobEncoder interface for gob serialization.

<a name="Decimal.GreaterThan"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [GreaterThan](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1343>)

```go
func (d Decimal) GreaterThan(d2 Decimal) bool
```

GreaterThan \(GT\) returns true when d is greater than d2.

<a name="Decimal.GreaterThanOrEqual"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [GreaterThanOrEqual](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1348>)

```go
func (d Decimal) GreaterThanOrEqual(d2 Decimal) bool
```

GreaterThanOrEqual \(GTE\) returns true when d is greater than or equal to d2.

<a name="Decimal.InexactFloat64"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [InexactFloat64](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1459>)

```go
func (d Decimal) InexactFloat64() float64
```

InexactFloat64 returns the nearest float64 value for d. It doesn't indicate if the returned value represents d exactly.

<a name="Decimal.IntPart"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [IntPart](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1418>)

```go
func (d Decimal) IntPart() int64
```

IntPart returns the integer component of the decimal.

<a name="Decimal.IsInteger"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [IsInteger](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1282>)

```go
func (d Decimal) IsInteger() bool
```

IsInteger returns true when decimal can be represented as an integer value, otherwise, it returns false.

<a name="Decimal.IsNegative"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [IsNegative](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1387>)

```go
func (d Decimal) IsNegative() bool
```

IsNegative return

```
true if d < 0
false if d == 0
false if d > 0
```

<a name="Decimal.IsPositive"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [IsPositive](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1378>)

```go
func (d Decimal) IsPositive() bool
```

IsPositive return

```
true if d > 0
false if d == 0
false if d < 0
```

<a name="Decimal.IsZero"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [IsZero](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1396>)

```go
func (d Decimal) IsZero() bool
```

IsZero return

```
true if d == 0
false if d > 0
false if d < 0
```

<a name="Decimal.LessThan"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [LessThan](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1354>)

```go
func (d Decimal) LessThan(d2 Decimal) bool
```

LessThan \(LT\) returns true when d is less than d2.

<a name="Decimal.LessThanOrEqual"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [LessThanOrEqual](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1359>)

```go
func (d Decimal) LessThanOrEqual(d2 Decimal) bool
```

LessThanOrEqual \(LTE\) returns true when d is less than or equal to d2.

<a name="Decimal.Ln"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Ln](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1137>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Ln(precision int32) (Decimal, error)
```

Ln calculates natural logarithm of d. Precision argument specifies how precise the result must be \(number of digits after decimal point\). Negative precision is allowed.

Example:

```
d1, err := NewFromFloat(13.3).Ln(2)
d1.String()  // output: "2.59"

d2, err := NewFromFloat(579.161).Ln(10)
d2.String()  // output: "6.3615805046"
```

<a name="Decimal.MarshalBinary"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [MarshalBinary](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1815>)

```go
func (d Decimal) MarshalBinary() (data []byte, err error)
```

MarshalBinary implements the encoding.BinaryMarshaler interface.

<a name="Decimal.MarshalJSON"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [MarshalJSON](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1783>)

```go
func (d Decimal) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalJSON implements the json.Marshaler interface.

<a name="Decimal.MarshalText"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [MarshalText](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1891>)

```go
func (d Decimal) MarshalText() (text []byte, err error)
```

MarshalText implements the encoding.TextMarshaler interface for XML serialization.

<a name="Decimal.Mod"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Mod](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L689>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Mod(d2 Decimal) Decimal
```

Mod returns d % d2.

<a name="Decimal.Mul"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Mul](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L580>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Mul(d2 Decimal) Decimal
```

Mul returns d \* d2.

<a name="Decimal.Neg"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Neg](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L571>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Neg() Decimal
```

Neg returns \-d.

<a name="Decimal.NumDigits"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [NumDigits](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1255>)

```go
func (d Decimal) NumDigits() int
```

NumDigits returns the number of digits of the decimal coefficient \(d.Value\)

<a name="Decimal.Pow"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Pow](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L714>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Pow(d2 Decimal) Decimal
```

Pow returns d to the power of d2. When exponent is negative the returned decimal will have maximum precision of PowPrecisionNegativeExponent places after decimal point.

Pow returns 0 \(zero\-value of Decimal\) instead of error for power operation edge cases, to handle those edge cases use PowWithPrecision Edge cases not handled by Pow:

- 0 \*\* 0 =\> undefined value
- 0 \*\* y, where y \< 0 =\> infinity
- x \*\* y, where x \< 0 and y is non\-integer decimal =\> imaginary value

Example:

```
d1 := decimal.NewFromFloat(4.0)
d2 := decimal.NewFromFloat(4.0)
res1 := d1.Pow(d2)
res1.String() // output: "256"

d3 := decimal.NewFromFloat(5.0)
d4 := decimal.NewFromFloat(5.73)
res2 := d3.Pow(d4)
res2.String() // output: "10118.08037125"
```

<a name="Decimal.PowBigInt"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [PowBigInt](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L928>)

```go
func (d Decimal) PowBigInt(exp *big.Int) (Decimal, error)
```

PowBigInt returns d to the power of exp, where exp is big.Int. Only returns error when d and exp is 0, thus result is undefined.

When exponent is negative the returned decimal will have maximum precision of PowPrecisionNegativeExponent places after decimal point.

Example:

```
d1, err := decimal.NewFromFloat(3.0).PowBigInt(big.NewInt(3))
d1.String() // output: "27"

d2, err := decimal.NewFromFloat(629.25).PowBigInt(big.NewInt(5))
d2.String() // output: "98654323103449.5673828125"
```

<a name="Decimal.PowInt32"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [PowInt32](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L888>)

```go
func (d Decimal) PowInt32(exp int32) (Decimal, error)
```

PowInt32 returns d to the power of exp, where exp is int32. Only returns error when d and exp is 0, thus result is undefined.

When exponent is negative the returned decimal will have maximum precision of PowPrecisionNegativeExponent places after decimal point.

Example:

```
d1, err := decimal.NewFromFloat(4.0).PowInt32(4)
d1.String() // output: "256"

d2, err := decimal.NewFromFloat(3.13).PowInt32(5)
d2.String() // output: "300.4150512793"
```

<a name="Decimal.PowWithPrecision"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [PowWithPrecision](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L807>)

```go
func (d Decimal) PowWithPrecision(d2 Decimal, precision int32) (Decimal, error)
```

PowWithPrecision returns d to the power of d2. Precision parameter specifies minimum precision of the result \(digits after decimal point\). Returned decimal is not rounded to 'precision' places after decimal point.

PowWithPrecision returns error when:

- 0 \*\* 0 =\> undefined value
- 0 \*\* y, where y \< 0 =\> infinity
- x \*\* y, where x \< 0 and y is non\-integer decimal =\> imaginary value

Example:

```
d1 := decimal.NewFromFloat(4.0)
d2 := decimal.NewFromFloat(4.0)
res1, err := d1.PowWithPrecision(d2, 2)
res1.String() // output: "256"

d3 := decimal.NewFromFloat(5.0)
d4 := decimal.NewFromFloat(5.73)
res2, err := d3.PowWithPrecision(d4, 5)
res2.String() // output: "10118.080371595015625"

d5 := decimal.NewFromFloat(-3.0)
d6 := decimal.NewFromFloat(-6.0)
res3, err := d5.PowWithPrecision(d6, 10)
res3.String() // output: "0.0013717421"
```

<a name="Decimal.QuoRem"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [QuoRem](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L620>)

```go
func (d Decimal) QuoRem(d2 Decimal, precision int32) (Decimal, Decimal)
```

QuoRem does division with remainder d.QuoRem\(d2,precision\) returns quotient q and remainder r such that

```
d = d2 * q + r, q an integer multiple of 10^(-precision)
0 <= r < abs(d2) * 10 ^(-precision) if d>=0
0 >= r > -abs(d2) * 10 ^(-precision) if d<0
```

Note that precision\<0 is allowed as input.

<a name="Decimal.Rat"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Rat](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1438>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Rat() *big.Rat
```

Rat returns a rational number representation of the decimal.

<a name="Decimal.Round"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Round](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1533>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Round(places int32) Decimal
```

Round rounds the decimal to places decimal places. If places \< 0, it will round the integer part to the nearest 10^\(\-places\).

Example:

```
NewFromFloat(5.45).Round(1).String() // output: "5.5"
NewFromFloat(545).Round(-1).String() // output: "550" (with AvoidScientificNotation, "5.5E2" otherwise)
```

<a name="Decimal.RoundBank"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [RoundBank](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1668>)

```go
func (d Decimal) RoundBank(places int32) Decimal
```

RoundBank rounds the decimal to places decimal places. If the final digit to round is equidistant from the nearest two integers the rounded value is taken as the even number

If places \< 0, it will round the integer part to the nearest 10^\(\-places\).

Examples:

```
NewFromFloat(5.45).RoundBank(1).String() // output: "5.4"
NewFromFloat(545).RoundBank(-1).String() // output: "540"
NewFromFloat(5.46).RoundBank(1).String() // output: "5.5"
NewFromFloat(546).RoundBank(-1).String() // output: "550"
NewFromFloat(5.55).RoundBank(1).String() // output: "5.6"
NewFromFloat(555).RoundBank(-1).String() // output: "560"
```

<a name="Decimal.RoundCash"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [RoundCash](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1697>)

```go
func (d Decimal) RoundCash(interval uint8) Decimal
```

RoundCash aka Cash/Penny/öre rounding rounds decimal to a specific interval. The amount payable for a cash transaction is rounded to the nearest multiple of the minimum currency unit available. The following intervals are available: 5, 10, 25, 50 and 100; any other number throws a panic.

```
5:   5 cent rounding 3.43 => 3.45
 10:  10 cent rounding 3.45 => 3.50 (5 gets rounded up)
 25:  25 cent rounding 3.41 => 3.50
 50:  50 cent rounding 3.75 => 4.00
100: 100 cent rounding 3.50 => 4.00
```

For more details: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cash_rounding

<a name="Decimal.RoundCeil"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [RoundCeil](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1565>)

```go
func (d Decimal) RoundCeil(places int32) Decimal
```

RoundCeil rounds the decimal towards \+infinity.

Example:

```
NewFromFloat(545).RoundCeil(-2).String()   // output: "600"
NewFromFloat(500).RoundCeil(-2).String()   // output: "500"
NewFromFloat(1.1001).RoundCeil(2).String() // output: "1.11"
NewFromFloat(-1.454).RoundCeil(1).String() // output: "-1.4"
```

<a name="Decimal.RoundDown"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [RoundDown](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1642>)

```go
func (d Decimal) RoundDown(places int32) Decimal
```

RoundDown rounds the decimal towards zero.

Example:

```
NewFromFloat(545).RoundDown(-2).String()   // output: "500"
NewFromFloat(-500).RoundDown(-2).String()   // output: "-500"
NewFromFloat(1.1001).RoundDown(2).String() // output: "1.1"
NewFromFloat(-1.454).RoundDown(1).String() // output: "-1.4"
```

<a name="Decimal.RoundFloor"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [RoundFloor](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1590>)

```go
func (d Decimal) RoundFloor(places int32) Decimal
```

RoundFloor rounds the decimal towards \-infinity.

Example:

```
NewFromFloat(545).RoundFloor(-2).String()   // output: "500"
NewFromFloat(-500).RoundFloor(-2).String()   // output: "-500"
NewFromFloat(1.1001).RoundFloor(2).String() // output: "1.1"
NewFromFloat(-1.454).RoundFloor(1).String() // output: "-1.5"
```

<a name="Decimal.RoundUp"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [RoundUp](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1615>)

```go
func (d Decimal) RoundUp(places int32) Decimal
```

RoundUp rounds the decimal away from zero.

Example:

```
NewFromFloat(545).RoundUp(-2).String()   // output: "600"
NewFromFloat(500).RoundUp(-2).String()   // output: "500"
NewFromFloat(1.1001).RoundUp(2).String() // output: "1.11"
NewFromFloat(-1.454).RoundUp(1).String() // output: "-1.5"
```

<a name="Decimal.Scan"></a>

### func \(\*Decimal\) [Scan](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1831>)

```go
func (d *Decimal) Scan(value interface{}) error
```

Scan implements the sql.Scanner interface for database deserialization.

<a name="Decimal.ScientificNotationString"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [ScientificNotationString](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1980>)

```go
func (d Decimal) ScientificNotationString() string
```

ScientificNotationString serializes the decimal into standard scientific notation.

The notation is normalized to have one non\-zero digit followed by a decimal point and the remaining significant digits followed by "E" and the base\-10 exponent.

A zero, which has no significant digits, is simply serialized to "0".

<a name="Decimal.Shift"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Shift](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L599>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Shift(shift int32) Decimal
```

Shift shifts the decimal in base 10. It shifts left when shift is positive and right if shift is negative. In simpler terms, the given value for shift is added to the exponent of the decimal.

<a name="Decimal.Sign"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Sign](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1369>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Sign() int
```

Sign returns:

```
-1 if d <  0
 0 if d == 0
+1 if d >  0
```

<a name="Decimal.Sin"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Sin](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L2232>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Sin() Decimal
```

Sin returns the sine of the radian argument x.

<a name="Decimal.String"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [String](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1475>)

```go
func (d Decimal) String() string
```

String returns the string representation of the decimal with the fixed point.

Example:

```
d := New(-12345, -3)
println(d.String())
```

Output:

```
-12.345
```

<a name="Decimal.StringFixed"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [StringFixed](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1493>)

```go
func (d Decimal) StringFixed(places int32) string
```

StringFixed returns a rounded fixed\-point string with places digits after the decimal point.

Example:

```
NewFromFloat(0).StringFixed(2) // output: "0.00"
NewFromFloat(0).StringFixed(0) // output: "0"
NewFromFloat(5.45).StringFixed(0) // output: "5"
NewFromFloat(5.45).StringFixed(1) // output: "5.5"
NewFromFloat(5.45).StringFixed(2) // output: "5.45"
NewFromFloat(5.45).StringFixed(3) // output: "5.450"
NewFromFloat(545).StringFixed(-1) // output: "540"
```

Regardless of the \`AvoidScientificNotation\` option, the returned string will never be in scientific notation.

<a name="Decimal.StringFixedBank"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [StringFixedBank](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1512>)

```go
func (d Decimal) StringFixedBank(places int32) string
```

StringFixedBank returns a banker rounded fixed\-point string with places digits after the decimal point.

Example:

```
NewFromFloat(0).StringFixedBank(2) // output: "0.00"
NewFromFloat(0).StringFixedBank(0) // output: "0"
NewFromFloat(5.45).StringFixedBank(0) // output: "5"
NewFromFloat(5.45).StringFixedBank(1) // output: "5.4"
NewFromFloat(5.45).StringFixedBank(2) // output: "5.45"
NewFromFloat(5.45).StringFixedBank(3) // output: "5.450"
NewFromFloat(545).StringFixedBank(-1) // output: "540"
```

Regardless of the \`AvoidScientificNotation\` option, the returned string will never be in scientific notation.

<a name="Decimal.StringFixedCash"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [StringFixedCash](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1521>)

```go
func (d Decimal) StringFixedCash(interval uint8) string
```

StringFixedCash returns a Swedish/Cash rounded fixed\-point string. For more details see the documentation at function RoundCash.

Regardless of the \`AvoidScientificNotation\` option, the returned string will never be in scientific notation.

<a name="Decimal.StringScaled"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [StringScaled](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1918>)

```go
func (d Decimal) StringScaled(exp int32) string
```

StringScaled first scales the decimal then calls .String\(\) on it.

Deprecated: buggy and unintuitive. Use StringFixed instead.

<a name="Decimal.Sub"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Sub](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L560>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Sub(d2 Decimal) Decimal
```

Sub returns d \- d2.

<a name="Decimal.Tan"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Tan](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L2348>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Tan() Decimal
```

Tan returns the tangent of the radian argument x.

<a name="Decimal.Truncate"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Truncate](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1761>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Truncate(precision int32) Decimal
```

Truncate truncates off digits from the number, without rounding.

NOTE: precision is the last digit that will not be truncated \(must be \>= 0\).

Example:

```
decimal.NewFromString("123.456").Truncate(2).String() // "123.45"
```

<a name="Decimal.UnmarshalBinary"></a>

### func \(\*Decimal\) [UnmarshalBinary](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1795>)

```go
func (d *Decimal) UnmarshalBinary(data []byte) error
```

UnmarshalBinary implements the encoding.BinaryUnmarshaler interface. As a string representation is already used when encoding to text, this method stores that string as \[\]byte

<a name="Decimal.UnmarshalJSON"></a>

### func \(\*Decimal\) [UnmarshalJSON](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1769>)

```go
func (d *Decimal) UnmarshalJSON(decimalBytes []byte) error
```

UnmarshalJSON implements the json.Unmarshaler interface.

<a name="Decimal.UnmarshalText"></a>

### func \(\*Decimal\) [UnmarshalText](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1877>)

```go
func (d *Decimal) UnmarshalText(text []byte) error
```

UnmarshalText implements the encoding.TextUnmarshaler interface for XML deserialization.

<a name="Decimal.Value"></a>

### func \(Decimal\) [Value](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L1871>)

```go
func (d Decimal) Value() (driver.Value, error)
```

Value implements the driver.Valuer interface for database serialization.

<a name="NullDecimal"></a>

## type [NullDecimal](<https://github.com/shopspring/decimal/blob/master/decimal.go#L2072-L2075>)

NullDecimal represents a nullable decimal with compatibility for scanning null values from the database.

```go
type NullDecimal struct {
    Decimal Decimal
    Valid   bool
}
```

